---
title: "Statistiche di ricerca"
description: "Metriche live — citazioni, download, impatto"
lang: it
format: html
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false

library(cranlogs)
library(scholar)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(htmltools)

# ---- Modern Scholar palette (mirrors theme.scss) ----
col_ink     <- "#0F172A"   # slate ink (primary)
col_accent  <- "#1E4D8C"   # oxford blue
col_paper   <- "#FCFCFD"   # near-white surface
col_paper2  <- "#F1F4F8"
col_rule    <- "#E3E8EF"   # cool gray hairline
col_muted   <- "#5B6B82"   # slate muted
col_teal    <- "#0E7C7B"   # secondary — teal
col_amber   <- "#C7791A"   # secondary — amber
col_slate   <- "#3F5E8C"   # secondary — mid slate-blue
col_plum    <- "#6D3B6B"   # secondary — plum

# Base font family for plotly charts (matches site body font)
chart_font_family <- "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"

# Chart colors — ink + accent + cohesive scholarly secondary tones
chart_colors <- c(col_ink, col_accent, col_teal, col_slate, col_amber, col_plum, col_muted)

# ---- Google Scholar data (with local cache) ----
scholar_id <- "Qu66YZQAAAAJ"
cache_file <- "scholar_cache.json"

profile <- tryCatch({
  p <- get_profile(scholar_id)
  if (is.list(p) && "total_cites" %in% names(p)) p else stop("invalid")
}, error = function(e) NULL)

cite_history <- tryCatch({
  ch <- get_citation_history(scholar_id)
  if (is.data.frame(ch) && nrow(ch) > 0) ch else stop("invalid")
}, error = function(e) NULL)

# If Scholar API succeeded, save cache for CI use
if (!is.null(profile) && !is.null(cite_history)) {
  jsonlite::write_json(
    list(profile = profile, cite_history = cite_history),
    cache_file, auto_unbox = TRUE, pretty = TRUE
  )
}

# If Scholar API failed, load from cache
if (is.null(profile) || is.null(cite_history)) {
  if (file.exists(cache_file)) {
    cache <- jsonlite::read_json(cache_file, simplifyVector = TRUE)
    if (is.null(profile)) profile <- cache$profile
    if (is.null(cite_history)) cite_history <- as.data.frame(cache$cite_history)
  }
}
```

[Google Scholar · CRAN logs · aggiornamento automatico]{.eyebrow}

<p class="lede">Metriche di ricerca live — citazioni, indice H e download dei pacchetti CRAN — aggiornate automaticamente a ogni render del sito.</p>

## Metriche Google Scholar

```{r}
#| label: scholar-metrics
#| echo: false

if (!is.null(profile)) {
  total_cites <- profile$total_cites
  h_index <- profile$h_index
  i10_index <- profile$i10_index
} else {
  total_cites <- "N/D"
  h_index <- "N/D"
  i10_index <- "N/D"
}

# Editorial-style card: paper background, thin ink border, display-serif number, mono label.
# Styling lives in theme.scss under .ed-card.
make_card <- function(value, label, accent = FALSE) {
  div(
    class = paste0("ed-card", if (accent) " ed-card--accent" else ""),
    span(class = "ed-card__num", value),
    span(class = "ed-card__label", label)
  )
}

div(
  style = "display:flex;justify-content:center;gap:1.25rem;flex-wrap:wrap;margin:1.5rem 0 2rem;",
  make_card(format(total_cites, big.mark = ","), "Citazioni totali", accent = TRUE),
  make_card(h_index, "Indice H"),
  make_card(i10_index, "Indice i10")
)
```

### Citazioni per anno

```{r}
#| label: citations-per-year
#| echo: false

if (!is.null(cite_history)) {
  plot_ly(cite_history, x = ~year, y = ~cites, type = "bar",
          marker = list(color = col_accent,
                        line = list(color = col_ink, width = 0.5)),
          hovertemplate = "Anno: %{x}<br>Citazioni: %{y:,}<extra></extra>") |>
    layout(
      font = list(family = chart_font_family, color = col_ink, size = 13),
      xaxis = list(title = "Anno", dtick = 1,
                   gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
      yaxis = list(title = "Citazioni",
                   gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
      plot_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
      paper_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
      margin = list(t = 10, l = 60, r = 20, b = 50)
    )
} else {
  cat("Cronologia delle citazioni attualmente non disponibile.")
}
```

### Citazioni cumulate

```{r}
#| label: cumulative-citations
#| echo: false

if (!is.null(cite_history)) {
  cite_history$cumulative <- cumsum(cite_history$cites)

  plot_ly(cite_history, x = ~year, y = ~cumulative, type = "scatter", mode = "lines+markers",
          line = list(color = col_ink, width = 2.5),
          marker = list(color = col_accent, size = 7,
                        line = list(color = col_ink, width = 1)),
          hovertemplate = "Anno: %{x}<br>Cumulate: %{y:,}<extra></extra>") |>
    layout(
      font = list(family = chart_font_family, color = col_ink, size = 13),
      xaxis = list(title = "Anno", dtick = 2,
                   gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
      yaxis = list(title = "Citazioni cumulate",
                   gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
      plot_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
      paper_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
      margin = list(t = 10, l = 60, r = 20, b = 50)
    )
} else {
  cat("Cronologia delle citazioni attualmente non disponibile.")
}
```

---

## Download dei pacchetti CRAN

```{r}
#| label: cran-downloads
#| echo: false

my_packages <- c("bibliometrix", "openalexR", "dimensionsR", "pubmedR", "e2tree", "contentanalysis", "tall")

totals <- lapply(my_packages, function(pkg) {
  tryCatch({
    dl <- cran_downloads(pkg, from = "2015-01-01", to = Sys.Date())
    data.frame(package = pkg, total = sum(dl$count, na.rm = TRUE))
  }, error = function(e) {
    data.frame(package = pkg, total = 0)
  })
})
totals_df <- bind_rows(totals) |> arrange(desc(total))
```

### Download totali da CRAN

```{r}
#| label: download-cards
#| echo: false

# Editorial package card: smaller than ed-card, paper background, thin border.
make_dl_card <- function(value, label, highlight = FALSE) {
  div(
    class = paste0("ed-card", if (highlight) " ed-card--accent" else ""),
    style = "padding:1rem 1.15rem;min-width:0;",
    tags$span(class = "ed-card__num",
              style = "font-size:1.45rem;margin-bottom:.25rem;",
              value),
    tags$span(class = "ed-card__label",
              style = "font-size:.65rem;letter-spacing:.18em;",
              label)
  )
}

cards <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(totals_df)), function(i) {
  # First card (largest package = bibliometrix) gets the accent treatment
  make_dl_card(format(totals_df$total[i], big.mark = ","),
               totals_df$package[i],
               highlight = (i == 1))
})

div(
  style = "max-width:900px;margin:1.5rem auto 2.5rem;",
  div(
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:.75rem;margin-bottom:.75rem;",
    tagList(cards[1:min(4, length(cards))])
  ),
  div(
    style = "display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr);gap:.75rem;max-width:680px;margin:0 auto;",
    tagList(cards[5:length(cards)])
  )
)
```

### Download cumulati nel tempo

```{r}
#| label: cumulative-downloads
#| echo: false

monthly_data <- lapply(seq_along(my_packages), function(i) {
  pkg <- my_packages[i]
  tryCatch({
    dl <- cran_downloads(pkg, from = "2015-01-01", to = Sys.Date())
    dl$month <- format(dl$date, "%Y-%m")
    monthly <- dl |> group_by(month) |> summarise(downloads = sum(count), .groups = "drop")
    monthly$cumulative <- cumsum(monthly$downloads)
    monthly$package <- pkg
    monthly$date <- as.Date(paste0(monthly$month, "-01"))
    monthly
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
})

monthly_all <- bind_rows(monthly_data)

if (nrow(monthly_all) > 0) {
  p <- plot_ly()
  for (i in seq_along(unique(monthly_all$package))) {
    pkg <- unique(monthly_all$package)[i]
    d <- monthly_all |> filter(package == pkg)
    col <- chart_colors[((i - 1) %% length(chart_colors)) + 1]
    p <- p |> add_trace(data = d, x = ~date, y = ~cumulative,
                        type = "scatter", mode = "lines",
                        name = pkg,
                        line = list(width = 2.5, color = col),
                        hovertemplate = paste0(pkg, "<br>%{x|%b %Y}<br>Cumulati: %{y:,}<extra></extra>"))
  }
  p |> layout(
    font = list(family = chart_font_family, color = col_ink, size = 13),
    xaxis = list(title = "Data",
                 gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
    yaxis = list(title = "Download cumulati",
                 gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = -0.15),
    plot_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    paper_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    margin = list(t = 10, l = 60, r = 20, b = 70)
  )
}
```

### Download mensili (ultimi 12 mesi)

```{r}
#| label: monthly-downloads-recent
#| echo: false

recent_data <- lapply(seq_along(my_packages), function(i) {
  pkg <- my_packages[i]
  tryCatch({
    dl <- cran_downloads(pkg, from = Sys.Date() - 365, to = Sys.Date())
    dl$month <- format(dl$date, "%Y-%m")
    monthly <- dl |> group_by(month) |> summarise(downloads = sum(count), .groups = "drop")
    monthly$package <- pkg
    monthly$date <- as.Date(paste0(monthly$month, "-01"))
    monthly
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
})

recent_all <- bind_rows(recent_data)

if (nrow(recent_all) > 0) {
  p <- plot_ly()
  for (i in seq_along(my_packages)) {
    pkg <- my_packages[i]
    d <- recent_all |> filter(package == pkg)
    col <- chart_colors[((i - 1) %% length(chart_colors)) + 1]
    p <- p |> add_trace(data = d, x = ~date, y = ~downloads,
                        type = "bar", name = pkg,
                        marker = list(color = col),
                        hovertemplate = paste0(pkg, "<br>%{x|%b %Y}<br>Download: %{y:,}<extra></extra>"))
  }
  p |> layout(
    font = list(family = chart_font_family, color = col_ink, size = 13),
    barmode = "group",
    xaxis = list(title = "Mese",
                 gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
    yaxis = list(title = "Download",
                 gridcolor = col_rule, zerolinecolor = col_rule),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0.5, xanchor = "center", y = -0.15),
    plot_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    paper_bgcolor = "rgba(0,0,0,0)",
    margin = list(t = 10, l = 60, r = 20, b = 70)
  )
}
```

---

[Colophon · Fonte citazioni: Google Scholar · Fonte download: CRAN logs · Aggiornato automaticamente a ogni render]{.eyebrow style="display:block;text-align:center;margin-top:2rem;"}
